channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Teamgroup 2TB Pro+ MicroSDXC Card Review: High-Capacity and Performance
item.description: The Teamgroup 2TB Pro+ microSDXC UHS-I/U3 card is a high-capacity and performance memory card bundled with a USB Type-C Ultra CR I card reader. We review its features, speed, and reliability in this article.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI Releases Latest Model in Reasoning Series: O3-Mini
item.description: OpenAI releases its latest model, o3-mini, in ChatGPT and API, advancing the boundaries of small models and delivering exceptional STEM capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Capital One’s new AI agent will help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help consumers with one of the most frustrating, time-consuming processes in life: buying a car. The banking giant’s Chat Concierge provides information, makes decisions, and takes action using multiple AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: VMware offers fixes to severe vulnerabilities in VMware Aria
item.description: VMware has fixed multiple high-severity vulnerabilities affecting its cloud management platform (CMP), VMware Aria, which could allow attackers to steal sensitive credentials from the virtualization giant’s IT management and logging solutions.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: The Dangers of Artificial Intelligence Companions to Kids - A Warning from Parents
item.description: The tech industry's latest innovation, artificial intelligence companions, is putting children at risk with their untested and potentially harmful technology.
item.dc:creator: Robert C. Fellmeth, The Mercury News

item.title: If you were bored watching videos at a plodding 2x speed, you’re in luck: They can go faster
item.description: MrBeast’s fast-cutting, nonstop dramatics are already quick enough. Now, some users can watch his videos (and others) at 4x speed. YouTube is continuing its push for pace. In a new experiment, Premium users will be able to quadruple the speed of its videos.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Java-based organizations mostly use Java for AI development – report
item.description: Azul Systems, in its Azul 2025 State of Java Survey & Report, says that Java leads Python and JavaScript for AI development in Java shops, based on a survey of 2,039 Java professionals on six continents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
